name: Cancun
tagline: Turquoise water, white sand, all-inclusive luxury
description: Cancun is one of the world's most popular destination wedding locations — combining stunning Caribbean beaches with world-class all-inclusive resorts, easy international flights, and a well-established wedding industry. The Hotel Zone stretches 25 km along the coast, giving couples an enormous range of venue options from intimate boutique hotels to mega-resorts hosting hundreds of guests.
legalNotes: Mexico allows foreigners to legally marry. A civil ceremony performed by a licensed judge is required. You need valid passports, birth certificates (apostilled and translated to Spanish), tourist cards, and a blood test for HIV and syphilis done in Mexico within 14 days of the ceremony. Four witnesses with valid ID must be present. Most all-inclusive resorts handle the paperwork and coordination with local authorities.

microDestinations.name: Hotel Zone
microDestinations.description: 25 km strip of world-class resorts on a narrow peninsula between the Caribbean and Nichupte Lagoon
microDestinations.bestFor: Large weddings with 50+ guests who want all-inclusive convenience

microDestinations.name: Playa Mujeres
microDestinations.description: Newer, quieter resort area north of the Hotel Zone with pristine beaches and fewer crowds
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want Cancun accessibility without the spring break energy

microDestinations.name: Isla Mujeres
microDestinations.description: Charming island 20 minutes by ferry with colorful streets, golf cart transportation, and laid-back Caribbean feel
microDestinations.bestFor: Intimate weddings under 30 guests who want authentic island character

resortAffiliates.name: Sandals Royal Cancun
resortAffiliates.description: Adults-only luxury resort with butler service and overwater bungalows. Perfect for intimate ceremonies.

resortAffiliates.name: Excellence Playa Mujeres
resortAffiliates.description: Stunning adults-only resort on a private peninsula with dedicated wedding coordinators.

resortAffiliates.name: Hyatt Zilara Cancun
resortAffiliates.description: Sophisticated adults-only resort in the Hotel Zone with panoramic ocean views.

name: Punta Cana
tagline: Palm-fringed beaches and world-class resorts
description: Punta Cana sits at the easternmost tip of the Dominican Republic, boasting 48 km of white sand beaches and over 50 world-class resorts. It consistently ranks as one of the top Caribbean destination wedding locations for its combination of value, accessibility, and sheer beauty. The weather is exceptionally stable compared to other Caribbean destinations.
legalNotes: The Dominican Republic allows foreigners to legally marry with civil ceremonies. Paperwork requirements include authenticated birth certificates and proof of single status, all translated to Spanish. Documents must be apostilled. Most resorts offer wedding packages that handle the coordination with local authorities.

microDestinations.name: Bavaro
microDestinations.description: The heart of resort country — long stretches of white sand beach lined with all-inclusive mega-resorts
microDestinations.bestFor: Large weddings with guests who want full resort amenities

microDestinations.name: Cap Cana
microDestinations.description: Gated luxury enclave with private marinas, golf courses, and ultra-high-end resorts
microDestinations.bestFor: Upscale weddings where exclusivity matters

microDestinations.name: Uvero Alto
microDestinations.description: Less-developed stretch north of Bavaro with wilder beaches and fewer crowds
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want seclusion without sacrificing resort quality

resortAffiliates.name: Secrets Royal Beach Punta Cana
resortAffiliates.description: Adults-only luxury resort with stunning beach ceremony locations and dedicated wedding teams.

resortAffiliates.name: Paradisus Palma Real
resortAffiliates.description: AAA Five Diamond resort with a spectacular wedding gazebo right on the beach.

name: Jamaica
tagline: Lush mountains meet crystal-clear sea
description: Jamaica combines dramatic tropical landscapes with gorgeous beaches, offering one of the most scenic Caribbean wedding backdrops. From the cliffs of Negril to the lush gardens of Montego Bay, Jamaica's diversity of settings suits every couple's vision. The island has a long history of destination weddings and an excellent support infrastructure.
legalNotes: Jamaica makes it easy to legally marry — couples must be in the country for at least 24 hours before the ceremony. You need a valid passport, proof of divorce or death of spouse if applicable, and an official birth certificate. No blood test required. The resort wedding coordinator typically handles all arrangements with the local marriage officer.

microDestinations.name: Negril
microDestinations.description: Seven Mile Beach, Rick's Cafe cliff diving, and legendary sunsets — the island's laid-back party capital
microDestinations.bestFor: Fun-loving couples who want barefoot beach vibes and vibrant nightlife for guests

microDestinations.name: Montego Bay
microDestinations.description: Jamaica's main resort corridor with the most international flights and widest range of all-inclusive options
microDestinations.bestFor: Large weddings where ease of access for guests is the priority

microDestinations.name: Ocho Rios
microDestinations.description: Lush gardens, Dunn's River Falls, and dramatic jungle-to-sea scenery on the north coast
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want dramatic natural backdrops and excursion options for guests

microDestinations.name: Port Antonio
microDestinations.description: Unspoiled, lush, and remote — the 'real Jamaica' that most tourists never see
microDestinations.bestFor: Intimate elopements and small weddings where seclusion and authenticity matter

resortAffiliates.name: Sandals Negril
resortAffiliates.description: Set on seven miles of the world-famous Seven Mile Beach with dedicated wedding butler service.

resortAffiliates.name: Couples Tower Isle
resortAffiliates.description: Romantic couples-only resort on its own private island — accessible by boat.

name: Hawaii
tagline: Volcanic drama and tropical paradise
description: Hawaii combines the ease of a domestic destination (no passports for US couples, familiar legal system) with the otherworldly beauty of volcanic beaches, lush rainforests, and dramatic coastlines. Each island offers a distinct character: Maui for luxury and romance, Kauai for dramatic natural scenery, Oahu for cosmopolitan energy, and the Big Island for adventurous couples.
legalNotes: As a US state, Hawaii follows US marriage law. No waiting period, no residency requirement, no blood test. Both parties must apply in person for a marriage license at a Hawaii State Department of Health office. The license costs $60 and is valid for 30 days. Any licensed officiant can perform the ceremony.

microDestinations.name: Maui
microDestinations.description: The gold standard for Hawaiian weddings — Wailea's luxury resorts, Ka'anapali Beach, and Haleakala sunrise
microDestinations.bestFor: Classic luxury Hawaiian weddings with the widest range of venues

microDestinations.name: Kauai
microDestinations.description: The 'Garden Isle' — dramatic Na Pali cliffs, lush rainforest, and the most naturally stunning scenery in Hawaii
microDestinations.bestFor: Adventurous couples who want jaw-dropping natural backdrops

microDestinations.name: Oahu
microDestinations.description: Honolulu energy plus North Shore surf culture — the most accessible and cosmopolitan island
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want urban amenities plus beach beauty, and easy flights for guests

microDestinations.name: Big Island
microDestinations.description: Active volcanoes, black sand beaches, and stargazing at Mauna Kea — wild and diverse landscapes
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want something truly different with nature-first venues

resortAffiliates.name: Four Seasons Maui at Wailea
resortAffiliates.description: The gold standard for Hawaiian luxury weddings — impeccable service on Maui's best beach.

resortAffiliates.name: Grand Hyatt Kauai
resortAffiliates.description: Set on Poipu Beach with dramatic cliffs and gardens — stunning ceremony locations.

name: Bali
tagline: Spiritual beauty and exotic romance
description: Bali is a dream destination for couples who want something extraordinary — ancient temples, terraced rice paddies, dramatic clifftop venues, and a deeply spiritual culture that infuses every ceremony with meaning. Ubud offers jungle villas and temple settings; Seminyak and Uluwatu provide clifftop oceanfront drama. Bali is also exceptional value — world-class venues and vendors at a fraction of Western prices.
legalNotes: Indonesian law requires both parties to share one of six officially recognized religions (Islam, Catholicism, Protestantism, Hinduism, Buddhism, Confucianism) to legally marry. In practice, most foreign couples cannot legally marry in Bali. About 80% of international couples opt for a symbolic ceremony in Bali and handle the legal marriage at home — a courthouse ceremony costs $50-$100 in most US states. This approach gives you complete freedom with venue, officiant, and ceremony style.

microDestinations.name: Uluwatu
microDestinations.description: Dramatic limestone cliffs, world-class surf, and clifftop temples above the Indian Ocean
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want the most dramatic ceremony backdrop Bali offers

microDestinations.name: Ubud
microDestinations.description: Lush jungle interior — terraced rice paddies, sacred monkey forest, and spiritual energy
microDestinations.bestFor: Nature-loving couples who want a spiritual, jungle-surrounded ceremony

microDestinations.name: Seminyak
microDestinations.description: Bali's trendy beach district — boutique hotels, sunset beach clubs, and designer shops
microDestinations.bestFor: Style-conscious couples who want beach ceremonies with easy nightlife for guests

microDestinations.name: Nusa Dua
microDestinations.description: Manicured resort enclave on the southeast coast with calm waters and luxury properties
microDestinations.bestFor: Larger weddings where resort amenities and predictability matter most

resortAffiliates.name: COMO Uma Ubud
resortAffiliates.description: Intimate luxury resort in the jungle with a stunning clifftop ceremony location.

resortAffiliates.name: The Mulia, Nusa Dua
resortAffiliates.description: Sprawling beachfront resort with world-class wedding facilities — ideal for larger guest lists.

name: Santorini
tagline: Iconic sunsets over the caldera
description: Santorini is perhaps the most iconic backdrop in the world — the blue-domed churches of Oia, the sheer white caldera cliffs, the dramatic sunsets over the Aegean Sea. It's a bucket-list destination for a reason, but it requires advance planning: top venues book 12-18 months out, and summer crowds (June-August) can feel overwhelming. Shoulder season (May, September, October) offers the best balance of weather, light, and atmosphere.
legalNotes: Non-EU couples can legally marry in Greece but the process is complex: you must file with the local municipality at least 30 days in advance, provide authenticated documents (birth certificates, passports, proof of single status) all translated into Greek by a certified translator, and may need additional consular certificates. Many couples opt for a symbolic ceremony in Santorini and handle the legal marriage at home, which gives full flexibility on venue and officiant.

microDestinations.name: Oia
microDestinations.description: The postcard — blue domes, white caves, and the most photographed sunset on Earth
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want the iconic Santorini backdrop (book 12-18 months early)

microDestinations.name: Fira
microDestinations.description: The island capital perched on the caldera rim — more venues, restaurants, and nightlife than Oia
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want caldera views plus easier logistics and more guest options

microDestinations.name: Akrotiri
microDestinations.description: The quieter south — ancient ruins, red and black sand beaches, and lighthouse sunsets without crowds
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want Santorini magic without the Oia crowds

resortAffiliates.name: Andronis Luxury Suites
resortAffiliates.description: Cliff-top suites with infinity pools and direct caldera views — intimate and incredibly romantic.

resortAffiliates.name: Grace Santorini
resortAffiliates.description: Award-winning hotel with a spectacular infinity pool and the best caldera views on the island.

name: Tulum
tagline: Bohemian chic meets ancient Mayan coast
description: Tulum has exploded as a wedding destination for couples who want something beyond the traditional all-inclusive — think barefoot luxury on white sand cenotes, bohemian jungle villas, and a backdrop that blends ancient Mayan ruins with the turquoise Caribbean. It's the destination for free-spirited, design-conscious couples. Note: Tulum has fewer large resorts than Cancun, making it better suited for intimate weddings (under 50 guests).
legalNotes: Same legal requirements as Cancun — Mexico requires a civil ceremony performed by a licensed judge. You need valid passports, apostilled birth certificates translated to Spanish, and a blood test done in Mexico within 14 days of the ceremony. Four witnesses required. Most Tulum wedding venues work with local civil judges to manage the paperwork.

microDestinations.name: Tulum Beach Zone
microDestinations.description: The famous stretch of boutique jungle hotels along the white sand Caribbean coast
microDestinations.bestFor: Instagram-worthy barefoot ceremonies at design-forward boutique hotels

microDestinations.name: Cenote Venues
microDestinations.description: Sacred Mayan sinkholes with crystal-clear fresh water surrounded by jungle — utterly unique ceremony sites
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want a truly one-of-a-kind ceremony setting

microDestinations.name: Sian Ka'an Reserve
microDestinations.description: UNESCO biosphere reserve south of Tulum — wild mangroves, ruins, and pristine coastline
microDestinations.bestFor: Ultra-private elopements in nature

resortAffiliates.name: Azulik Tulum
resortAffiliates.description: Adult-only eco-luxury resort suspended above the jungle canopy — utterly unique and Instagram-famous.

resortAffiliates.name: Habitas Tulum
resortAffiliates.description: Soulful boutique resort focused on wellness and community — beautiful for intimate ceremonies.

name: Key West
tagline: Barefoot elegance, sunset views, and Old Florida charm
description: Key West is America's most romantic domestic destination wedding location — a tiny island at the tip of Florida with a laid-back Caribbean vibe, extraordinary sunsets over the Gulf of Mexico, and a deep culture of celebration. The famous Duval Street energy gives way to quiet historic streets lined with Victorian mansions, private garden estates, and beachfront venues that feel worlds apart from the mainland. No passport required, easy to reach, and endlessly photogenic.
legalNotes: Key West follows Florida state marriage law — couples need a valid government-issued photo ID and Social Security number. No waiting period for non-Florida residents and no blood test required. Marriage license is valid for 60 days from issue date. Florida residents must wait 3 days after issue (waivable with a premarital course). License fee is approximately $86.

microDestinations.name: Old Town
microDestinations.description: Historic district with Victorian mansions, lush garden venues, and the iconic Mallory Square sunset
microDestinations.bestFor: Garden weddings with historic architecture and walkable charm

microDestinations.name: Sunset Key
microDestinations.description: Private island accessible only by ferry — the most exclusive venue in Key West
microDestinations.bestFor: Intimate luxury weddings where privacy and exclusivity matter

microDestinations.name: Stock Island
microDestinations.description: The artsy, slightly grittier neighbor with waterfront venues and creative energy
microDestinations.bestFor: Budget-conscious couples who want Key West character without premium prices

resortAffiliates.name: Sunset Key Cottages
resortAffiliates.description: Private island cottages accessible only by ferry — the most exclusive wedding venue in Key West with dramatic Gulf sunset views.

resortAffiliates.name: Casa Marina Key West
resortAffiliates.description: Historic 1920s beachfront resort with Key West's largest private beach and grand ballroom.

resortAffiliates.name: The Reach Key West
resortAffiliates.description: Curio Collection resort directly on the beach with dedicated wedding coordinators and stunning sunset ceremony spots.

name: Costa Rica
tagline: Rainforest, volcanoes, and Pacific beaches
description: Costa Rica is for adventurous couples who want a wedding surrounded by extraordinary natural beauty — howler monkeys, tropical birds, active volcanoes, and jungle waterfalls, all minutes from pristine Pacific beaches. The Guanacaste and Nicoya Peninsula region (dry season accessible) is the most popular for destination weddings, with excellent resort infrastructure and stunning sunset views.
legalNotes: Costa Rica allows foreign nationals to legally marry. The ceremony must be performed by a licensed notary public or authorized religious celebrant. You need apostilled copies of birth certificates and, if previously married, proof of divorce. No blood test or residency requirement. Most wedding planners handle all document requirements and notary coordination.

microDestinations.name: Guanacaste
microDestinations.description: The 'Gold Coast' — Pacific dry forest, world-class resorts, and the most predictable dry season in Costa Rica
microDestinations.bestFor: Larger weddings where reliable weather and resort infrastructure matter

microDestinations.name: Nicoya Peninsula
microDestinations.description: Surf towns, yoga retreats, and wild Pacific beaches — Santa Teresa and Nosara
microDestinations.bestFor: Free-spirited couples who want a wellness-oriented, barefoot celebration

microDestinations.name: Manuel Antonio
microDestinations.description: National park where rainforest meets the ocean — monkeys, sloths, and dramatic jungle-beach scenery
microDestinations.bestFor: Nature-loving couples who want wildlife and jungle as their ceremony backdrop

microDestinations.name: Arenal
microDestinations.description: Active volcano, hot springs, and cloud forest — the most dramatic inland setting in Costa Rica
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want a non-beach destination with volcano views and adventure activities for guests

resortAffiliates.name: Four Seasons Peninsula Papagayo
resortAffiliates.description: Spectacular resort on its own private peninsula — two beaches, marina, and exceptional wedding services.

resortAffiliates.name: Andaz Costa Rica Resort
resortAffiliates.description: Hyatt's stunning clifftop resort with panoramic ocean views and a dedicated wedding team.

name: Los Cabos
tagline: Desert drama where the Pacific meets the Sea of Cortez
description: Los Cabos sits at the tip of Baja California where the Pacific Ocean collides with the Sea of Cortez — creating a landscape unlike anywhere else in Mexico. Think desert cliffs plunging into azure water, El Arco's famous rock arch, and sunsets that set the sky on fire. The area has exploded with luxury resorts and is now one of the top 5 destination wedding markets for US couples. The vibe is distinctly different from Caribbean Mexico — more dramatic, more sophisticated, more cinematic.
legalNotes: Los Cabos is in Baja California Sur, which follows Mexican marriage law. A civil ceremony by a licensed judge is required. You need valid passports, apostilled birth certificates translated to Spanish, tourist cards, and a blood test done in Mexico within 14 days of the ceremony. Four witnesses with valid ID are required. One year must have lapsed since any prior divorce. Most resorts coordinate the legal process.

microDestinations.name: Cabo San Lucas
microDestinations.description: The lively side — El Arco, marina, nightlife, and the famous arch rock formations
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want dramatic Pacific ceremony backdrops and vibrant guest entertainment

microDestinations.name: San Jose del Cabo
microDestinations.description: The quieter, artsy side — colonial architecture, art galleries, and a charming downtown
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who prefer cultural charm and sophistication over party energy

microDestinations.name: The Corridor
microDestinations.description: 20-mile stretch of luxury resorts between the two Cabos — cliffside venues with Pacific views
microDestinations.bestFor: Luxury resort weddings where the resort IS the destination

microDestinations.name: East Cape
microDestinations.description: Undeveloped coastline along the Sea of Cortez — raw, wild, and increasingly discovered
microDestinations.bestFor: Adventurous couples who want off-the-beaten-path Baja beauty

resortAffiliates.name: Waldorf Astoria Los Cabos Pedregal
resortAffiliates.description: Ultra-luxury resort accessed through a private tunnel carved into the mountain. Iconic arch views from every suite.

resortAffiliates.name: One&Only Palmilla
resortAffiliates.description: The original Los Cabos luxury resort — 27 acres of beachfront with a legendary wedding reputation.

resortAffiliates.name: Esperanza, Auberge Resorts
resortAffiliates.description: Cliffside resort on the Corridor with some of the most dramatic ceremony views in all of Mexico.

name: St. Lucia
tagline: Volcanic peaks rising from turquoise Caribbean waters
description: St. Lucia offers what no other Caribbean island can — the Pitons. These twin volcanic peaks rising dramatically from the sea create the most visually striking wedding backdrop in the Caribbean. Beyond the Pitons, the island delivers lush rainforest, sulfur springs, and a sophisticated resort scene anchored by properties like Jade Mountain and Sugar Beach. It's pricier than other Caribbean options but the photography alone is worth it.
legalNotes: St. Lucia allows foreigners to legally marry. No residency period — you can obtain a marriage license within 24 hours of arrival with a same-day application (EC $540 fee) or a regular license after 2 days (EC $335). Required documents: valid passport, birth certificate, and proof of single status. If divorced or widowed, bring a decree absolute or death certificate. No blood test required. Documents not in English must be authenticated and translated.

microDestinations.name: Soufriere
microDestinations.description: The Pitons district — Jade Mountain, Sugar Beach, sulfur springs, and the most dramatic views on the island
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want the iconic Pitons as their ceremony backdrop

microDestinations.name: Rodney Bay
microDestinations.description: The tourism hub in the north — more accessible, more restaurants and nightlife, Reduit Beach
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want easier logistics and more guest entertainment options

microDestinations.name: Marigot Bay
microDestinations.description: Sheltered yacht harbor surrounded by steep jungle hillsides — one of the most beautiful bays in the Caribbean
microDestinations.bestFor: Intimate, exclusive weddings in a picture-perfect harbor setting

resortAffiliates.name: Jade Mountain
resortAffiliates.description: Open-walled suites with private infinity pools and direct Piton views — the most iconic wedding venue in the Caribbean.

resortAffiliates.name: Sugar Beach, A Viceroy Resort
resortAffiliates.description: Set between the two Pitons on a white sand beach — dramatic rainforest-to-beach ceremony settings.

resortAffiliates.name: Sandals Grande St. Lucian
resortAffiliates.description: All-inclusive on its own peninsula with dedicated WeddingMoon packages and multiple ceremony locations.

name: Riviera Maya
tagline: Caribbean luxury between Cancun and Tulum
description: The Riviera Maya stretches along 80 miles of Caribbean coastline between Cancun and Tulum, anchored by Playa del Carmen. It offers the best of both worlds — the resort infrastructure and accessibility of Cancun with more character and less spring-break energy. The area is packed with excellent all-inclusive resorts, many with dedicated wedding departments that handle hundreds of ceremonies per year. Cenotes (freshwater sinkholes) provide truly unique ceremony venues.
legalNotes: Same requirements as Cancun — Riviera Maya is in Quintana Roo. Civil ceremony by a licensed judge, valid passports, apostilled birth certificates translated to Spanish, blood test in Mexico within 14 days of the ceremony, four witnesses required. Most resorts handle the full legal process.

microDestinations.name: Playa del Carmen
microDestinations.description: The vibrant heart of the Riviera Maya — Fifth Avenue shops, restaurants, beach clubs, and cosmopolitan energy
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want resort convenience plus walkable town nightlife for guests

microDestinations.name: Puerto Aventuras
microDestinations.description: Gated resort community with private marina, dolphin encounters, and family-friendly amenities
microDestinations.bestFor: Family-focused weddings with children where resort safety and activities matter

microDestinations.name: Akumal
microDestinations.description: Sea turtle nesting grounds and calm snorkeling bays — quieter and more nature-focused than Playa
microDestinations.bestFor: Nature-loving couples who want a quieter, wildlife-rich setting

resortAffiliates.name: Grand Velas Riviera Maya
resortAffiliates.description: Ultra-luxury all-inclusive with three distinct ambiances: zen, ambassador, and grand class. Award-winning wedding program.

resortAffiliates.name: Rosewood Mayakoba
resortAffiliates.description: Secluded luxury resort set along lagoons and cenotes in a private nature reserve. The most exclusive address on the Riviera Maya.

name: Turks & Caicos
tagline: Grace Bay — the world's most pristine beach
description: Turks & Caicos has one thing no other Caribbean destination can claim: Grace Bay Beach, consistently voted the world's best beach. The water is an almost impossible shade of turquoise, the sand is powdery white, and the resort scene is distinctly upscale without being pretentious. It's a smaller, quieter, more exclusive alternative to the all-inclusive mega-resorts of Cancun or Punta Cana — perfect for couples who want Caribbean beauty without the crowds.
legalNotes: Turks & Caicos allows foreigners to legally marry. You must be present in the islands for at least 48 hours before applying for a marriage license. Required documents: valid passports, notarized copies of birth certificates, and proof of single status. If divorced, a certified decree absolute is needed. Marriage license costs US $250 from the Registrar General's Office in Providenciales. Two witnesses (other than the officiant) must be present. Note: same-sex marriage is not legally recognized in TCI.

microDestinations.name: Grace Bay
microDestinations.description: The world-famous beach — 12 miles of powder-white sand and the island's best resorts
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want the world's best beach as their ceremony backdrop

microDestinations.name: Chalk Sound
microDestinations.description: Stunning turquoise lagoon dotted with tiny rocky islands — one of the most photogenic spots in the Caribbean
microDestinations.bestFor: Intimate elopements and ceremonies where dramatic color is everything

microDestinations.name: Parrot Cay
microDestinations.description: Private island resort accessible only by boat — ultimate exclusivity and privacy
microDestinations.bestFor: Ultra-private, celebrity-level destination weddings

resortAffiliates.name: Grace Bay Club
resortAffiliates.description: The original Grace Bay luxury resort — all-suite property with dedicated wedding team and stunning beachfront ceremony locations.

resortAffiliates.name: The Shore Club
resortAffiliates.description: Contemporary luxury resort on Long Bay Beach with dramatic infinity pool and sophisticated ceremony settings.

name: Aruba
tagline: Sunny, dry, and outside the hurricane belt
description: Aruba's biggest selling point is something no other Caribbean destination can match: it sits below the hurricane belt. While other islands track storms from June to November, Aruba enjoys year-round sunshine with minimal rain. The island has a distinct landscape — desert cacti, divi-divi trees, and dramatic limestone coastline alongside white sand beaches. It's a reliable choice for couples who refuse to gamble on weather.
legalNotes: Aruba (part of the Kingdom of the Netherlands) allows foreigners to legally marry. The civil ceremony must take place at the historic city hall in Oranjestad. Required documents: long-form birth certificates (apostilled), valid passports, certificate of no impediment (apostilled, not older than 6 months), and completed Declaration of Marriage Intent and Single Status forms. All documents must be submitted at least one month before the wedding date. Two witnesses aged 18+ required. If divorced, a complete divorce decree is needed.

microDestinations.name: Eagle Beach
microDestinations.description: Wide, powdery white sand beach with the famous fofoti divi-divi trees — consistently rated a top world beach
microDestinations.bestFor: Classic beach ceremony with the iconic Aruba divi-divi tree photos

microDestinations.name: Palm Beach
microDestinations.description: The main resort strip — high-rise hotels, water sports, restaurants, and nightlife
microDestinations.bestFor: Larger weddings where guest entertainment and resort convenience matter

microDestinations.name: Arikok National Park
microDestinations.description: Rugged desert landscape with cacti, natural bridges, and hidden coves on the windward coast
microDestinations.bestFor: Adventurous couples who want a dramatically different ceremony backdrop

resortAffiliates.name: The Ritz-Carlton, Aruba
resortAffiliates.description: Luxury resort on Palm Beach with dedicated wedding team, multiple ceremony locations, and impeccable service.

resortAffiliates.name: Bucuti & Tara Beach Resort
resortAffiliates.description: Adults-only boutique resort on Eagle Beach — intimate, romantic, and certified carbon-neutral.

name: Amalfi Coast
tagline: Cliffside villages cascading to the Mediterranean
description: The Amalfi Coast is Italy's most dramatic wedding backdrop — pastel-colored villages clinging to sheer cliffs above the Mediterranean, terraced lemon groves, and winding coastal roads that reveal new breathtaking views at every turn. Positano, Ravello, and Amalfi each offer distinct settings. This is one of the most aspirational and photographed wedding destinations in the world, and the venues match the scenery — historic villas, cliffside terraces, and gardens with panoramic sea views.
legalNotes: Foreigners can legally marry in Italy but the process takes 3-6 months of preparation. You need a 'Nulla Osta' (certificate of no impediment) from your home country's embassy in Italy, plus an Atto Notorio (sworn statement with two witnesses at a consulate), birth certificates translated and apostilled, and a formal declaration of intent at the local town hall. All documents must be translated into Italian. Given this complexity, most US couples get legally married at home first and hold a symbolic ceremony on the Amalfi Coast — this gives complete freedom with venue and ceremony style.

microDestinations.name: Ravello
microDestinations.description: Hilltop town 1,000 feet above the sea — Villa Rufolo and Villa Cimbrone are two of the most famous wedding venues in Italy
microDestinations.bestFor: Classic luxury wedding with panoramic terrace views and Old World elegance

microDestinations.name: Positano
microDestinations.description: The iconic pastel cascade — colorful buildings tumbling down to a pebble beach, the most photographed town on the coast
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want the most visually striking ceremony backdrop Italy offers

microDestinations.name: Amalfi
microDestinations.description: Historic maritime republic with a stunning 9th-century cathedral, piazza, and harbor setting
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want cultural depth and cathedral ceremony options

resortAffiliates.name: Belmond Hotel Caruso, Ravello
resortAffiliates.description: 11th-century palace with an infinity pool overlooking the coast and one of Italy's most famous wedding terraces.

resortAffiliates.name: Le Sirenuse, Positano
resortAffiliates.description: The most iconic boutique hotel in Positano — terrace dining, art collection, and perfect Positano views.

name: Tuscany
tagline: Rolling vineyards and Renaissance romance
description: Tuscany is the ultimate vineyard wedding destination — rolling hills dotted with cypress trees, medieval stone villages, world-class wine estates, and golden light that photographers dream about. The region offers everything from grand Renaissance villas to intimate farmhouse agriturismo settings. Unlike the Amalfi Coast, Tuscany is accessible by car, making multi-day celebrations with excursions (wine tours, cooking classes, Florence day trips) easy for guests.
legalNotes: Same Italian marriage law as the Amalfi Coast — a Nulla Osta from your embassy, Atto Notorio, translated/apostilled birth certificates, and declaration of intent at the local comune are required. The process takes 3-6 months. Most couples handle the legal ceremony at home and hold a symbolic celebration in Tuscany. This is especially common for vineyard estate weddings, which often prefer the flexibility of symbolic ceremonies.

microDestinations.name: Chianti
microDestinations.description: The wine country heartland — rolling vine-covered hills between Florence and Siena with centuries-old estates
microDestinations.bestFor: Classic vineyard weddings at wine estates with al fresco dining

microDestinations.name: Val d'Orcia
microDestinations.description: UNESCO-listed landscape — the iconic cypress-lined roads, golden fields, and medieval hill towns of southern Tuscany
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want the most photographed landscape in Italy as their backdrop

microDestinations.name: Florence
microDestinations.description: Renaissance city with world-class art, architecture, and rooftop venues overlooking the Duomo
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want cultural richness and city elegance combined with Tuscan style

microDestinations.name: Lucca
microDestinations.description: Walled medieval city with a more intimate, less touristy feel than Florence — garden villas and olive groves
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want Tuscan charm without the Florence crowds

resortAffiliates.name: Castello di Casole, A Belmond Hotel
resortAffiliates.description: 10th-century estate with 4,200 acres of Tuscan countryside — the most exclusive wedding venue in the region.

resortAffiliates.name: Rosewood Castiglion del Bosco
resortAffiliates.description: 800-year-old estate in the Val d'Orcia with its own Brunello vineyard and restored medieval borgo.

name: Algarve
tagline: Golden sea cliffs, warm culture, exceptional value
description: The Algarve is Portugal's southern coast — dramatic golden limestone cliffs, hidden sea caves, pristine beaches, and a warm, welcoming culture that makes every guest feel at home. It offers Mediterranean beauty at a fraction of Italian or Greek prices, with excellent infrastructure, direct flights from major US hubs, and a rapidly growing wedding industry. The food and wine are world-class. This is the destination for couples who want European elegance without the European price tag.
legalNotes: Portugal allows foreigners to legally marry with no residency requirement. You need valid passports, birth certificates (issued within 6 months, translated to Portuguese, apostilled), and a Certificate of No Impediment from your home country. The application process takes 4-6 weeks before the ceremony. Beach weddings require permits from maritime police and local council. Many couples opt for a symbolic ceremony for greater venue flexibility and handle the legal paperwork at home.

microDestinations.name: Lagos
microDestinations.description: The western Algarve — dramatic Ponta da Piedade sea cliffs, grottos, and a charming old town
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want the most dramatic cliffside ceremony photos in Portugal

microDestinations.name: Albufeira
microDestinations.description: The main resort hub — old town charm plus beach clubs, restaurants, and nightlife for guests
microDestinations.bestFor: Larger weddings where guest entertainment and dining options matter

microDestinations.name: Vilamoura
microDestinations.description: Upscale marina town with golf courses, yacht harbor, and luxury resort properties
microDestinations.bestFor: Sophisticated couples who want marina views and luxury resort amenities

microDestinations.name: Tavira
microDestinations.description: The quieter eastern Algarve — Roman bridges, tile-roofed houses, and island beaches reached by ferry
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want authentic Portuguese character without the tourist crowds

resortAffiliates.name: Pine Cliffs Resort
resortAffiliates.description: Clifftop luxury resort above a golden beach with multiple ceremony locations including a dramatic cliff-edge terrace.

resortAffiliates.name: Vila Vita Parc
resortAffiliates.description: Award-winning resort on 54 acres of clifftop gardens with a private beach, wine cellar, and dedicated wedding team.

name: Dubrovnik
tagline: Medieval walls above the sparkling Adriatic
description: Dubrovnik is the Adriatic's crown jewel — a UNESCO-listed walled city perched above crystal-clear waters with terracotta roofs, limestone streets, and centuries of history in every stone. Made globally famous as a filming location, the city delivers a cinematic wedding backdrop that feels like stepping into another era. Beyond the old town, the surrounding coastline offers island-hopping, secluded beaches, and dramatic cliffside venues. Summer (July-August) is very crowded; shoulder season is ideal.
legalNotes: Foreigners can legally marry in Croatia with no residency requirement, but documentation must be submitted 30-45 days before the ceremony. You need original birth certificates and Certificate of No Impediment (both less than 90 days old), translated into Croatian and apostilled. A certified court interpreter must be present if neither partner speaks Croatian. Civil ceremonies can be held at the registry office or approved off-site locations for an extra fee. Given the paperwork and interpreter requirements, many couples opt for a symbolic ceremony and handle the legal marriage at home.

microDestinations.name: Old Town
microDestinations.description: The UNESCO-walled city itself — limestone streets, baroque churches, and fortress views over the Adriatic
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want the most cinematic historic setting in the Mediterranean

microDestinations.name: Elafiti Islands
microDestinations.description: Quiet archipelago 20 minutes by ferry — car-free Lopud and Sipan islands with hidden coves
microDestinations.bestFor: Intimate island weddings with ferry-arrival drama for guests

microDestinations.name: Cavtat
microDestinations.description: Charming harbor town 20 minutes south — less crowded, more local, with waterfront venues
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want Dubrovnik proximity without old town crowds

resortAffiliates.name: Hotel Excelsior Dubrovnik
resortAffiliates.description: Five-star hotel with direct views of the old town walls — the closest luxury property to the historic center.

resortAffiliates.name: Sun Gardens Dubrovnik
resortAffiliates.description: Sprawling resort on its own peninsula with multiple ceremony locations and a private beach.

name: Maldives
tagline: Overwater villas on crystal lagoons
description: The Maldives is the ultimate expression of tropical luxury — 1,192 coral islands scattered across the Indian Ocean, each resort occupying its own private island. Overwater bungalows, crystal-clear lagoons, bioluminescent beaches, and underwater restaurants make this a destination that defies comparison. Weddings here are intimate by nature (most resorts are small), making the Maldives perfect for elopements and micro-weddings where the focus is entirely on the couple.
legalNotes: Foreigners cannot legally marry in the Maldives. Marriage law is governed by Islamic Sharia, and legal ceremonies are restricted to Maldivian citizens. Every foreign couple 'married' in the Maldives has a symbolic ceremony — the resort provides a celebrant, you exchange vows, and the experience looks and feels exactly like a wedding, but carries no legal weight. Handle the legal paperwork at your local courthouse before or after your trip (most US courthouses charge $50-$100).

microDestinations.name: North Male Atoll
microDestinations.description: Closest to the airport — shortest seaplane or speedboat transfer, most resort options
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want to minimize travel time after the long flight

microDestinations.name: South Ari Atoll
microDestinations.description: Famous for whale shark encounters and some of the best diving in the world
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want underwater adventures alongside their ceremony

microDestinations.name: Baa Atoll
microDestinations.description: UNESCO biosphere reserve — pristine coral reefs and manta ray aggregation sites
microDestinations.bestFor: Eco-conscious couples who want the most pristine natural environment

resortAffiliates.name: Soneva Fushi
resortAffiliates.description: The original Maldives luxury island — barefoot luxury, private beaches, and legendary service on a private island.

resortAffiliates.name: One&Only Reethi Rah
resortAffiliates.description: One of the largest private islands in the Maldives — 12 beaches, overwater villas, and a dedicated wedding pavilion.

name: Fiji
tagline: South Pacific paradise with the warmest people on Earth
description: Fiji is the South Pacific dream — 333 islands with some of the world's clearest water, pristine coral reefs, and a culture so famously warm that 'Bula!' (welcome) is the first word every visitor learns. Destination weddings in Fiji benefit from world-class resorts, genuinely friendly local staff who make celebrations feel personal, and landscapes that range from volcanic peaks to palm-fringed atolls. It's farther from the US than Caribbean alternatives but the payoff is a truly exotic, uncrowded setting.
legalNotes: Fiji allows foreigners to legally marry. You need valid passports, birth certificates (original or certified), Certificate of Single Status (or Certificate of No Impediment), and proof of any prior divorce or widowhood. The marriage license must be obtained from a Births, Deaths & Marriages office — allow at least 3 days before the ceremony. License is valid for 28 days. Only Fijian celebrants can legally solemnize the marriage. Two witnesses aged 21+ are required. Marriage certificate receives an Apostille Seal for international recognition.

microDestinations.name: Mamanuca Islands
microDestinations.description: The classic Fiji postcard — palm-fringed islands near Nadi with the easiest access and most resort options
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want classic South Pacific beauty with shortest transfer times

microDestinations.name: Yasawa Islands
microDestinations.description: More remote volcanic islands with pristine beaches and far fewer tourists
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want Robinson Crusoe seclusion and raw natural beauty

microDestinations.name: Coral Coast
microDestinations.description: The main island's southern coast — longer sandy beaches, coral reefs, and cultural village visits
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want mainland convenience plus authentic Fijian cultural experiences

resortAffiliates.name: Laucala Island
resortAffiliates.description: Ultra-private island resort owned by Red Bull's founder — 25 villas across 3,500 acres of pristine paradise.

resortAffiliates.name: Likuliku Lagoon Resort
resortAffiliates.description: Fiji's first overwater bungalows — adult-only, set on a pristine lagoon in the Mamanuca Islands.

name: Holbox Island
tagline: No cars, lagoons, flamingos — Tulum before it got discovered
description: Isla Holbox is what Tulum was 15 years ago — a car-free island off the Yucatan coast where you get around by golf cart, flamingos wade in the shallow lagoon, and bioluminescent plankton light up the water at night. The island has a handful of boutique hotels, no chain resorts, and a deeply relaxed energy that feels completely removed from the outside world. It's perfect for couples who want the Caribbean Mexico experience without the crowds, the Instagram influencers, or the $500/night hotel rooms.
legalNotes: Holbox is part of Quintana Roo state — same legal requirements as Cancun and Tulum. Civil ceremony by a licensed judge, passports, apostilled birth certificates translated to Spanish, blood test in Mexico within 14 days, four witnesses required. Logistics are slightly more complex given the island's remoteness (ferry access only), so coordinate with a local wedding planner well in advance.

resortAffiliates.name: Mystique Holbox by Royalton
resortAffiliates.description: The island's most upscale resort — beachfront with a pool, spa, and dedicated event space for intimate ceremonies.

name: Roatan
tagline: Caribbean water as clear as Cancun at 40% the cost
description: Roatan is the largest of Honduras's Bay Islands, sitting on the Mesoamerican Barrier Reef — the second-largest reef system in the world. The water clarity rivals anything in the Caribbean, the diving is world-class, and the cost of everything (venues, food, accommodation) is a fraction of what you'd pay in Cancun or the Turks & Caicos. The island is still under the radar for weddings, which means smaller crowds, more personal service, and venues that haven't been booked solid 18 months out.
legalNotes: Foreigners can legally marry in Honduras, but the process involves more bureaucracy than most Caribbean destinations. Requirements include birth certificates with civil status, an HIV test (if the couple has no children in common), a medical certificate, authentication at the Honduran consulate (~$80), and all documents translated to Spanish. Marriage license costs $140 (cash only). Given the paperwork and medical requirements, many couples opt for a symbolic ceremony in Roatan and handle the legal marriage at home.

microDestinations.name: West Bay
microDestinations.description: The island's best beach — white sand, clear water, and the most tourist-friendly strip of Roatan
microDestinations.bestFor: Beach ceremonies with the island's clearest water and easiest logistics

microDestinations.name: West End
microDestinations.description: Bohemian village with dive shops, beach bars, and colorful waterfront — the island's social hub
microDestinations.bestFor: Laid-back couples who want village character and walkable nightlife for guests

resortAffiliates.name: Infinity Bay Spa & Beach Resort
resortAffiliates.description: The island's premier wedding venue — beachfront condos, dedicated event coordinator, and West Bay Beach location.

name: Kotor
tagline: Adriatic fjord, medieval walls — Dubrovnik without the crowds
description: Kotor sits at the end of the Bay of Kotor — Europe's southernmost fjord — where dramatic mountains plunge into deep blue Adriatic water, and a UNESCO-listed medieval old town with Venetian architecture feels frozen in time. If Dubrovnik is the famous sibling, Kotor is the equally beautiful one that nobody has discovered yet. Wedding costs are roughly half of Dubrovnik, venues are easier to book, and the setting is arguably more dramatic. The bay offers extraordinary ceremony locations including the iconic Our Lady of the Rocks church on its own tiny island.
legalNotes: Foreigners can legally marry in Montenegro with no residency requirement. You need birth certificates and Certificate of No Impediment (both under 90 days old), translated into Montenegrin, apostilled, and submitted at least 7 days before the ceremony. Passports of both parties and witnesses are required. A certified court interpreter must be present if neither partner speaks Montenegrin. Civil marriages are performed by a registrar. Religious ceremonies alone are not legally binding — a civil wedding must take place first. Given the translation, apostille, and interpreter requirements, some couples prefer a symbolic ceremony.

microDestinations.name: Old Town Kotor
microDestinations.description: UNESCO-listed medieval walled town — Venetian palaces, sea gates, and fortification walks above the bay
microDestinations.bestFor: History-loving couples who want an intimate medieval setting

microDestinations.name: Perast
microDestinations.description: Tiny baroque village on the bay with Our Lady of the Rocks — a church on its own man-made island
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want the most unique ceremony venue in the Mediterranean (island church)

microDestinations.name: Tivat (Porto Montenegro)
microDestinations.description: Modern marina town with superyacht harbor, boutique hotels, and contemporary Mediterranean style
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want modern luxury with marina-front ceremony locations

resortAffiliates.name: Regent Porto Montenegro
resortAffiliates.description: Five-star hotel on the Porto Montenegro marina — contemporary luxury with mountain and bay views.

name: Azores
tagline: Volcanic crater lakes, dramatic cliffs — Europe's best-kept secret
description: The Azores are nine Portuguese islands in the middle of the Atlantic Ocean — volcanic crater lakes, lush green pastures, dramatic sea cliffs, and hot springs surrounded by some of the most dramatic natural scenery in Europe. Almost nobody thinks of the Azores for weddings, which is exactly why they're remarkable. Imagine exchanging vows on the rim of Sete Cidades, a twin volcanic lake of blue and green water, with zero other wedding parties in sight. Flights from the US East Coast are just 4.5 hours. Costs are a fraction of mainland Portugal.
legalNotes: The Azores follow Portuguese marriage law — foreigners can legally marry with no residency requirement. Birth certificates (issued within 3 months, translated to Portuguese, apostilled) and Certificate of No Impediment are required. Application takes 4-6 weeks minimum. Given the Azores' remoteness, most couples handle the legal ceremony at home and have a symbolic celebration on the islands for maximum flexibility.

microDestinations.name: Sao Miguel
microDestinations.description: The largest island — twin volcanic lakes of Sete Cidades, hot springs, tea plantations, and the most infrastructure
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want the most dramatic natural ceremony backdrop in Europe

microDestinations.name: Faial
microDestinations.description: The 'Blue Island' — hydrangea-lined roads, a volcanic caldera, and the famous Peter's Cafe Sport marina
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want a nautical, Atlantic-explorer energy

microDestinations.name: Flores
microDestinations.description: The most remote western island — waterfalls, volcanic lakes, and almost zero tourists
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want the most secluded, untouched setting in the Azores

resortAffiliates.name: Octant Furnas
resortAffiliates.description: Boutique hotel in the volcanic valley of Furnas — natural hot spring pools, lush gardens, and farm-to-table dining.

name: Koh Lanta
tagline: Authentic Thailand, quieter than Phuket, exceptional value
description: Koh Lanta is Thailand's quieter, more authentic alternative to Phuket and Koh Samui — a long, thin island in the Andaman Sea with sunset beaches on the west coast, mangrove forests, and a traditional fishing village (Old Town) that gives the island genuine Thai character. The wedding industry is smaller but growing, with boutique beach resorts that offer personalized ceremonies at prices that would be impossible in the US. Think $200 flower arrangements, $500 full-day photography, and five-star beachfront hotels under $200/night.
legalNotes: Foreigners can legally marry in Thailand, but the process requires visiting your home country's embassy in Bangkok to obtain an Affirmation of Freedom to Marry, translating it to Thai, legalizing at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, then registering at a district office. The process takes at least 2-3 working days and involves travel to Bangkok. Since Koh Lanta is remote, most couples have a symbolic ceremony on the island and handle the legal registration either at home or separately in Bangkok.

microDestinations.name: Long Beach
microDestinations.description: The island's longest beach on the west coast — golden sand, sunset views, and the most resort options
microDestinations.bestFor: Classic beach ceremony with the best sunset backdrop on the island

microDestinations.name: Old Town
microDestinations.description: Historic Lanta fishing village on stilts over the water — Chinese-Thai-Muslim heritage and genuine local life
microDestinations.bestFor: Couples who want cultural authenticity and unique waterfront character

microDestinations.name: Kantiang Bay
microDestinations.description: Secluded southern bay surrounded by jungle-covered hills — quieter and more private than Long Beach
microDestinations.bestFor: Intimate ceremonies where seclusion and natural beauty are the priority

resortAffiliates.name: Pimalai Resort & Spa
resortAffiliates.description: Five-star hillside resort set in 100 acres of tropical forest on Kantiang Bay — the most luxurious property on the island.
